subcommands.name: run
subcommands.description: [experimental] Run tasks

args.name: TASK
args.description: Tasks to run Can specify multiple tasks by separating with ':::'

args.name: ARGS
args.description: Arguments to pass to the tasks. Use ':::' to separate tasks

name: -n
--dry-run
options.description: Don't actually run the tasks(s), just print them in order of execution

name: -f
--force
options.description: Force the tasks to run even if outputs are up to date

name: -p
--prefix
options.description: Print stdout/stderr by line, prefixed with the tasks's label

name: -i
--interleave
options.description: Print directly to stdout/stderr instead of by line

name: -t
--tool
options.description: Tool(s) to also add e.g.: node@20 python@3.10

name: -v
--verbose
options.description: Show extra output (use -vv for even more)

name: -j
--jobs
options.description: Number of tasks to run in parallel

args.name: JOBS
args.description: Number of jobs to run in parallel

name: -y
--yes
options.description: Answer yes to all confirmation prompts

name: -r
--raw
options.description: Read/write directly to stdin/stdout/stderr instead of by line

name: --timings
options.description: Show elapsed time after each tasks
